symptoms.symptom_name: Acute nocturnal chest tightness and expiratory wheeze

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Expiratory wheeze with chest tightness in a known asthmatic is the core presentation of an exacerbation.
Cardiac wheeze ('cardiac asthma') is possible but less likely without cardiac history.
Poor response to a beta-agonist reliever indicates a severe exacerbation and is an ambulance trigger in asthma first aid.
Worse airflow obstruction and work of breathing when supine in severe bronchospasm.
Orthopnoea is a classic feature of left-heart failure and must be actively excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Adult-onset asthma with recurrent nasal polyps and allergic rhinitis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adult-onset asthma with chronic rhinosinusitis and nasal polyposis is the classic prodromal phenotype of EGPA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reported peripheral eosinophilia on recent bloods

loinc_ref.display: Eosinophils [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral eosinophilia is a defining feature of the eosinophilic phase of EGPA; needs formal FBC confirmation, not chat-obtainable in real time.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no purulent sputum, no pleuritic chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Infective exacerbation of asthma / lower respiratory tract infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever and purulent sputum makes an infective driver less likely.
No pleuritic chest pain; wheeze-dominant picture — PE less likely though not excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread polyphonic expiratory wheeze with good air entry, no silent chest, no accessory-muscle use (auscultation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reversible bronchospasm with preserved air entry; a silent chest or accessory-muscle use would signal life-threatening severity. Sealed — the AI Doctor cannot auscultate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Boggy nasal turbinates consistent with nasal polyps (ENT examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic rhinosinusitis with polyps supports the eosinophilic phenotype; requires ENT/clinician examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: SpO2 96% on room air, HR 102, RR 22, BP 135/85, afebrile (clinician-measured vitals)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Maintained SpO2 with sinus tachycardia (partly salbutamol/anxiety) indicates a moderate-to-severe but not yet hypoxaemic flare. Sealed — clinician-measured; the AI Doctor has no device reading and must not assume these values.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of peripheral neuropathy or purpuric rash

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No mononeuritis multiplex or palpable purpura — consistent with the pre-vasculitic (prodromal) phase rather than established systemic vasculitis.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Adult-onset asthma + chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps + peripheral eosinophilia together define the prodromal phase of EGPA and mandate urgent outpatient workup.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count with eosinophil differential
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies peripheral eosinophilia — the key objective clue to the eosinophilic phenotype.
ANCA (typically MPO/pANCA) supports EGPA and helps stratify vasculitis risk; arranged via GP/specialist.
Assesses for pulmonary infiltrates (eosinophilic or infective) and excludes other causes of acute breathlessness.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Peak expiratory flow / spirometry with reversibility (once stabilised)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objectively documents airflow obstruction and reversibility to guide preventer escalation — not to be performed acutely if it worsens distress.
PE is clinically unlikely (wheeze-dominant, no pleuritic pain); routine D-dimer here would be a low-yield over-investigation unless the clinical picture changes.
